paper_title: Investigating the leaching performance of ferric chloride solution for metallic alloys resulting from reduction smelting of spent lithium-ion batteries

entities.description: The process of recovering valuable metals from end-of-life batteries to ensure sustainable production and reduce environmental damage.
The efficiency and effectiveness of dissolving metals from alloys using a lixiviant solution.
A process that produces metallic alloys containing Co, Ni, Cu, Mn, and Fe from spent batteries.
The primary lixiviant (FeCl3) used for the dissolution of metals from metallic alloys.
Technology including leaching, solvent extraction, and precipitation for metal purification.
Equipment used to confirm the chemical structure of the metallic alloy powders.
Inductively coupled plasma-optical emission spectrometry used to determine metal content.
A chemical mixture used to completely dissolve powder samples for ICP-OES analysis.
Equipment used to stir reaction mixtures at 500 rpm.
Digital record of the chemical composition of the metallic alloy powders.
The most effective molar concentration for leaching metals.
The weight ratio of powder to solution achieving complete dissolution.
The reaction temperature identified as the best condition.
The duration required for complete metal dissolution.
The acidity of the solution after the leaching process under optimal conditions.
Research field focusing on the properties and recovery of metallic materials.
Researcher from Can Tho University of Technology.
Researcher from Can Tho University.
Researcher from Can Tho University.
Researcher from Mokpo National University.

entities.id: College of Engineering Technology, Can Tho University
entities.description: Faculty of Biological, Chemical and Food Technology, Viet Nam.
College of Engineering Technology and College of Natural Sciences, Viet Nam.
Institute of Rare Metal, Korea.
